note: Add full narrations here. The app picks 7 per day and shows 3 at a time with same-day rotation. Keep ids stable once shipped.

narrations.label: Firmness in Occultation
narrations.quote: Blessed are those who remain steadfast upon their faith in the time of the occultation of their Imam.
narrations.body: Steadfastness in occultation is not passive waiting. It is returning to prayer when distraction wins, returning to knowledge when ignorance spreads, and returning to hope when the wait feels long. The companions of the Qa'im are forged in this quiet discipline.

narrations.label: The Lamp of Vigil
narrations.quote: The believer in the time of occultation is like one who keeps vigil with a lamp that must not go out.
narrations.body: Your lamp is guarded by salah, knowledge, and mutual care. Tend it daily. The night of occultation is long, but dawn belongs to those who did not abandon their post.

narrations.label: Inner Refinement
narrations.quote: Prepare for that which is coming by refining that which is within.
narrations.body: Preparation is inward before it is outward. Cleanse intention, strengthen the body for service, and keep the heart awake for the call. Spectacle does not make companions; sincerity does.

narrations.label: Patience as Craft
narrations.quote: Patience during occultation is an active craft for those who await with certainty.
narrations.body: Patience is not numbness. It is choosing again each day: prayer on time, honesty in dealings, and refusal to trade conviction for ease. Craft it as you would any skill worth mastering.

narrations.label: Community Resilience
narrations.quote: Hold fast to the rope of Allah together, and do not become divided in the long night.
narrations.body: The occultation tests communities as much as individuals. Visit the lonely, teach the young, and protect the weak. A scattered ummah cannot recognize its Imam with one heart.

narrations.label: Knowledge Before the Call
narrations.quote: Seek understanding so that when the call rises, you are not among the confused.
narrations.body: Ignorance is a fog that thickens in occultation. Read, ask, and verify. The companions are people of insight — not rumor, not panic, not borrowed slogans.

narrations.label: Service in Silence
narrations.quote: The best of deeds in occultation are those done for Allah when no eye of praise is watching.
narrations.body: Hidden charity, private tears, and quiet discipline shape the soul for open service. What you practice unseen is what you will carry when the world suddenly sees.

narrations.label: Hope Without Haste
narrations.quote: Do not hasten the affair; rather prepare yourselves as you have been commanded.
narrations.body: Haste breeds despair and exaggeration. Hope is steady work: purify, train, learn, and wait with dignity. The timing belongs to Allah; the preparation belongs to you.

narrations.label: Heart Presence
narrations.quote: Let not your hearts harden in the length of the wait, for hardness is the beginning of loss.
narrations.body: Softness of heart is maintained by remembrance, du'a, and honest muhasaba. A hard heart cannot recognize truth even when it stands before it.

narrations.label: Companions of Certainty
narrations.quote: They are people of certainty whose resolve does not break when trials intensify.
narrations.body: Certainty is tested in hunger, fear, rumor, and delay. Strengthen the body, sharpen the mind, and polish the soul — so that when the trial peaks, your resolve is already trained.

narrations.label: Firmness in Occultation
narrations.quote: None of them will remain (on the right path) except a very few, who maintains on the religion of Allah, holds fast by the (rope) of Allah and do not deviate from the right path of Allah.
narrations.body: Steadfastness in occultation is not passive waiting. It requires maintaining one's religion despite the surrounding confusion and holding firmly to divine guidance.

narrations.label: The True Source of Religion
narrations.quote: He, who comes into this religion imitating the men, will be taken out from it by those men, who has taken him in, and he, who comes to it by the Book and the Sunna, mountains vanish before he gives up.
narrations.body: True faith must be anchored in the immutable truths of the Quran and the Prophetic tradition, rather than the shifting opinions of men.

narrations.label: Perception over Narration
narrations.quote: You know the positions of our followers near us according to the traditions they narrate from us and according to what extent they perceive from us.
narrations.body: Deep comprehension of the traditions is valued far above mere memorization or repetition. One understood tradition is better than a thousand repeated mindlessly.

narrations.label: Like Bees Among Birds
narrations.quote: Be among the people like the bees among the birds. All the birds deem them weak but if they know what there is in their interiors, they will not treat them so. Mix with people with your bodies and be away from them with your hearts.
narrations.body: A believer navigates society with outward gentleness while guarding a profound, inner spiritual resilience that remains unseen to the ignorant.

narrations.label: The Purifying Test
narrations.quote: By Allah, you will be tested. By Allah, you will be flown right and left until no one of you will remain except he, whom Allah has promised, fixed faith into his heart and assisted with a power of His.
narrations.body: Periods of great confusion serve as a divine sieve, separating those with wavering beliefs from those whose faith is divinely anchored.

narrations.label: The Fewer and Fewest
narrations.quote: ...until no one of you will keep on this matter except the fewer and the fewest.
narrations.body: The path of truth during the occultation is lonely. As trials intensify, only a dedicated minority will hold fast to the promise of the Mahdi.

narrations.label: Patience and Waiting
narrations.quote: Be patient in offering the obligations, be patient before your enemy and station yourselves in waiting for your expected imam!
narrations.body: Waiting for the Imam is an active state. It demands fulfilling religious duties, remaining resilient against opposition, and maintaining vigilant anticipation.

narrations.label: The Lonely Path
narrations.quote: Do not feel desolate in the way of guidance when there are very few people walking in it.
narrations.body: The validity of a path is not determined by the number of its travelers. Truth often remains the pursuit of the few.

narrations.label: Getting to the Source
narrations.quote: He, who walks in the (right) way, will get to the water (source) and he, who deviates from it, will get lost.
narrations.body: Adhering to the lineage of divine guidance guarantees spiritual sustenance, while turning away leads to inevitable confusion and spiritual thirst.

narrations.label: Arguing with the Infatuated
narrations.quote: Beware of arguing with any infatuated one because he is prompted (by the Satan) to a certain period and when his period passes, his sin will burn him.
narrations.body: Engaging in endless debates with those blinded by illusion is fruitless. Time ultimately reveals the destructive nature of their falsehoods.

narrations.label: Preserving the Secret
narrations.quote: Undertaking our matter is not by knowing and accepting it. It is by preserving and keeping it secret from those, who are not reliable.
narrations.body: True loyalty to the Imams involves profound discretion. Protecting their esoteric teachings from those who would mock or misuse them is a duty.

narrations.label: Silence and Submission
narrations.quote: It suffices you to say what we have said and to be silent on what we have been silent on.
narrations.body: Obedience requires mirroring the actions and restraints of the divine guides. Speaking when they spoke, and remaining silent when they withheld.

narrations.label: Spreading Secrets
narrations.quote: He, who spreads our (secret) traditions, is like one, who denies our right.
narrations.body: Exposing sacred knowledge to unprepared minds is as damaging as outright rejection of the Imams' divine authority.

narrations.label: The Parable of the Ark
narrations.quote: My family to you is like the Ark of Noah. Whoever rides on it will be safe and whoever lags behind it will drown.
narrations.body: In the flood of worldly confusion and spiritual peril, the Prophet's household serves as the solitary vessel of divine rescue.

narrations.label: The Gate of Forgiveness
narrations.quote: The example of my family to you is like the Gate of Forgiveness of the Israelites. Whoever enters it his sins will be forgiven and will deserve mercy and blessing of Allah.
narrations.body: Just as the Israelites were commanded to enter the Gate of Forgiveness to be absolved of their sins, the Muslim Ummah is directed to the Prophet's family as the gateway to divine mercy.

narrations.label: The Infallible Guides
narrations.quote: I and my family are infallible. Do not precede them in order not to go astray, do not lag behind them in order not to fall in mistakes, do not object to them in order not to be ignorant...
narrations.body: Following the Ahlul Bayt requires precise alignment—neither rushing ahead with personal innovations nor lagging behind in negligence. They are the supreme measure of the truth.

narrations.label: The Divine Covenant
narrations.quote: You may think that we determine the matter of the imamate as we like! By Allah, it is a covenant from Allah revealed to His messenger (as) and then to certain men one after the other until it reaches its keeper.
narrations.body: The Imamate is not a political institution subject to human desire or election. It is a strict divine covenant passed down through a designated lineage.

narrations.label: The Earth Never Empty
narrations.quote: The earth does never remain without a Hujjah; either apparent and known or afraid and unknown lest Allah's proofs and signs will be invalid.
narrations.body: The presence of a divine proof (Hujjah) is a cosmic necessity. Whether visible to the public or hidden in occultation, the Imam anchors the truth on earth.

narrations.label: Twelve Inspired Imams
narrations.quote: From among my progeny there will be twelve inspired men.
narrations.body: The number of the Imams was predetermined and announced by the Prophet himself. They are exclusively twelve, divinely guided and inspired to protect the faith.

narrations.label: The Hidden and the Apparent
narrations.quote: ...and then He completed the twelve imams from the progeny of al-Husayn. The ninth of them is the hidden and the apparent and the best of them. He is the expected imam.
narrations.body: The Mahdi holds a unique paradox: he is physically hidden from the eyes of the masses but his spiritual authority and guidance remain universally apparent.

narrations.label: The Inevitable Return
narrations.quote: He will be al-Mahdi, who will spread justice and equity allover the world after it has been filled with injustice and oppression. There will be confusion and disappearance for him.
narrations.body: The ultimate goal of the Mahdi's return is the restoration of universal justice, but it is necessarily preceded by a grueling period of occultation and widespread societal confusion.

narrations.label: The Continuous Guardians
narrations.quote: They are the guardians, one after the other, until they come to me at the pond (in Paradise). Every one of them is guided and he is a guide for people.
narrations.body: The unbroken chain of Imams provides constant spiritual nourishment and direction, seamlessly guiding the ummah until the Day of Judgment.

narrations.label: The Sifting of the Believers
narrations.quote: By Allah, you are going to be tested, clarified and sifted like sifting out the Zu'an from wheat.
narrations.body: The period of occultation serves as a divine sieve. Only the purest hearts, free from the chaff of doubt and worldly attachment, will endure the prolonged waiting.

narrations.label: Islam Returning Strange
narrations.quote: Islam has begun strange and it will return strange as it has begun. Blessed are the strangers!
narrations.body: When the Mahdi reappears, he will re-establish the pure, unadulterated Islam, which will seem entirely foreign to a world corrupted by generations of innovation.

narrations.label: The Constant Guide
narrations.quote: Each imam is a guide to the people of his age.
narrations.body: Divine guidance is never suspended. Every generation is endowed with a living Imam who perfectly understands and addresses the unique challenges of his era.

narrations.label: The Painful Ignorance
narrations.quote: He, who spent a night without knowing his imam, will die as an unbeliever.
narrations.body: Acknowledging the living Imam is not optional; it is the cornerstone of Islamic faith. Without this foundational connection, one's religious standing collapses into ignorance.

narrations.label: Unaccepted Worship
narrations.quote: Whoever tries his best in worshipping Allah without following an imam inspired by Allah, his worshipping will not be accepted and he is astray and confused...
narrations.body: Acts of worship must be directed through the proper divine channels. Diligence in prayer without loyalty to the appointed Imam is a misdirected and fruitless spiritual effort.

narrations.label: The Price of Denial
narrations.quote: He, who denies one of the alive imams, has denied the dead (imams).
narrations.body: The chain of Imamate is an indivisible whole. Rejecting the authority of the current Imam is equivalent to rejecting the entire prophetic and immamate lineage.

narrations.label: Fleeing from Personal Opinions
narrations.quote: It means one, who depends upon his own opinion in the affairs of religion without following an imam of the imams of guidance.
narrations.body: True religion relies on divine revelation and appointed guides, not on the shifting, fallible whims of personal opinion and analogical reasoning.

narrations.label: Two Disappearances
narrations.quote: Al-Qa'im disappears twice; one is a short disappearance and the other is long.
narrations.body: The occultation occurs in two phases. The minor occultation featured designated deputies, while the major occultation removes direct contact, severely testing the believers' patience.

narrations.label: The False Claimants
narrations.quote: There are three persons, whom Allah will not speak to on the Day of Resurrection... one, who pretends that he is an imam but he is not...
narrations.body: Claiming the divine right of Imamate without authorization is a profound spiritual crime, severing the claimant entirely from God's mercy on the Day of Judgment.

narrations.label: Preparing for the Return
narrations.quote: Every one of you is to prepare himself for the appearance of al-Qa'im even with one arrow...
narrations.body: Awaiting the Mahdi is not a passive state. It demands active physical, spiritual, and mental readiness, demonstrating a sincere commitment to his revolution.

narrations.label: The Danger of Haste
narrations.quote: The diviners tell lies. We, Ahlul Bayt, do not time. Allah dose not consent save to reverse the timing of the diviners.
narrations.body: Believers must avoid attempting to predict the exact date of the Mahdi's return. Such haste contradicts the required submission to God's ultimate and unrevealed timing.

narrations.label: The Heavenly Call
narrations.quote: A caller will call out from the Heaven that so-and-so is the emir and that Ali and his followers are the triumphants.
narrations.body: The advent of the Mahdi will be heralded by an undeniable cosmic sign—a divine voice from the sky affirming the truth of Ali and his followers, clearing all worldly ambiguities.

narrations.label: The Sword of Justice
narrations.quote: Al-Qa'im will rise with a new task, new principles and new judgements. He will be severe with the Arabs. He will do not but killing.
narrations.body: While the Prophet established the religion with leniency, the Mahdi's era is one of execution and final justice. He will fiercely eradicate entrenched corruption without compromise.

narrations.label: The 313 Companions
narrations.quote: Allah will make his (al-Mahdi's) three hundred and thirteen companions join him without any previous appointment.
narrations.body: The core commanders of the Mahdi's revolution are a predetermined, divinely selected elite. Their miraculous gathering in Mecca signifies the suddenness of God's command.

narrations.label: The Obscure Youth
narrations.quote: This matter (deliverance) will be carried out by the youngest and obscurest one of us.
narrations.body: The savior will rise from a place of profound obscurity and will be granted authority at a very young age, confounding the expectations of traditional power structures.

narrations.label: A Trial by Absence
narrations.quote: You still expect (deliverance) until Allah sends for this matter one, whom you do not know whether he is created or not.
narrations.body: The ultimate test of faith is believing in an Imam whose very existence is highly obscured and debated by the public, relying purely on the theological necessity of his presence.

narrations.label: The Purified Remainder
narrations.quote: By Allah you are going to be sifted, to be clarified and to be purified until none of you remain save the least.
narrations.body: The long occultation acts as a relentless crucible. As time stretches, superficial believers fall away, leaving only a highly concentrated, unbreakable core of devotees.

narrations.label: The Light of the Intellect
narrations.quote: Know your imam. If you know him, it does not harm you whether this matter comes soon or late...
narrations.body: Epistemological certainty in the Imam is the goal, not merely witnessing his earthly triumph. One who truly recognizes the Imam has already achieved spiritual deliverance.

narrations.label: A Shepherd to His Flock
narrations.quote: The world will not be empty of a jurisprudent (imam). Whenever the believers add something (a heresy) to the religion, he prevents them from that...
narrations.body: Even in occultation, the Imam actively preserves the integrity of the faith, subtly preventing the complete corruption of the religious tradition.

narrations.label: The Paradox of Visibility
narrations.quote: People will miss an imam. He attends the seasons (of hajj). He sees the people but they do not see him.
narrations.body: Occultation is not absence from the world. The Imam is physically present, observing his community and performing rituals, though veiled from their direct recognition.

narrations.label: The Prophetic Relics
narrations.quote: When al-Qa'im appears, he will appear with the banner of Prophet Muhammad (as), the ring of Solomon (as) and the rock and the staff of Moses (as).
narrations.body: The Mahdi is the inheritor of the spiritual and physical legacies of all major prophets, wielding their miraculous artifacts to establish ultimate divine authority.

narrations.label: The Call of Gabriel
narrations.quote: The call will make every sleeper awake, every stander sit and every sitter stand because of fright... the first cry will be the cry of Gabriel (as).
narrations.body: The cosmic announcement of the Mahdi's arrival will be a terrifying, awe-inspiring event, cutting through all human ignorance and forcing global attention to the truth.

narrations.label: The True Deliverance
narrations.quote: This matter (the appearance of al-Qa'im) has a precise time that it must reach. If it reaches its time, it will occur neither an hour sooner nor an hour later.
narrations.body: God's timeline for salvation is absolute and unalterable by human anxiety. Believers must cultivate patience rather than rushing a divine process that is meticulously scheduled.

narrations.label: The Banners of Guidance
narrations.quote: From among the three banners, only the banner of al-Yamani will be a banner of guidance because he will invite to the mission of your man (al-Qa'im).
narrations.body: In the chaos preceding the Mahdi, various political and military movements will arise. Only the movement strictly aligned with and paving the way for the Mahdi is considered legitimate guidance.

narrations.label: A Ruler from the Progeny
narrations.quote: The rule of al-Qa'im will last for nineteen years and some months.
narrations.body: Once the grueling wait is over, the Mahdi's reign, though brief in the grand scale of history, will fundamentally transform the earth with concentrated justice and equity.

narrations.label: Between Knowing and Denying
narrations.quote: When al-Qa'im (as) rises, people will deny him because he will come back to them as a young man.
narrations.body: The prolonged occultation will skew public expectations. When the Mahdi returns with miraculous youth despite his age, superficial believers will fail to recognize him.

narrations.label: The Test of the Fetus
narrations.quote: You are going to be tried with the fetus while still in its mother's womb and with the suckling until it is said: he has disappeared and he has died.
narrations.body: The occultation challenges believers from the very inception of the Mahdi's life. Trusting in a hidden birth and a concealed existence separates the resolute from the doubtful.

narrations.label: The Ultimate Relief
narrations.quote: If only two men remain on the earth, one of them must be al-Hujjah (Imam al-Mahdi).
narrations.body: The necessity of a divine guide is an immutable law of existence. Human life cannot persist without the tether of spiritual authority binding the earth to the heavens.
